item.title: Degenerate Toroidal Quasibound States in the Continuum Enabling Polarization-Independent Exciton-Polaritons
item.description:
Author(s): Xianyan Wei, Chaomian Wu, Yawei Chen, Jianhua Zeng, Weiyi Hong, and Fu Deng

Achieving polarization-independent strong light-matter coupling remains a central challenge for practical integrated photonic platforms. Conventional metasurfaces based on bound states in the continuum (BICs) can sustain ultrahigh quality ($Q$) factors, yet their intrinsic polarization sensitivity l…


[Phys. Rev. B 114, 045401] Published Wed Jul 01, 2026

item.title: Vibrational and Diffusional Thermal Transport in Amorphous and Deeply Supercooled Liquid Alumina
item.description:
Author(s): Honggang Zhang, Xiaoyi Chen, Yangshun Lan, Changchun Ding, Dingbo Zhang, Yuxiang Ni, and Hua Bao

Thermal transport in amorphous materials involves a complex interplay between anharmonicity and atomic-scale dynamics, particularly across the glass transition. To elucidate the underlying mechanisms, we investigate the temperature dependence of thermal transport in amorphous ${\mathrm{Al}}_{2}{\mat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 224318] Published Fri Jun 26, 2026

item.title: Stability of Lithium-Xenon Compounds at High Pressures
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaomeng Wang, Pei Zhou, Junjie Wang, Chi Ding, Qing Lu, Yu Han, Yang Ni, and Zhijie Cao

Using crystal structure prediction and first-principles calculations, we show that the Li-Xe system exhibits diverse structural, electronic, and dynamical behaviors under pressure. Lithium-rich phases display electride character, with interstitial electrons dominating the states at the Fermi level, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 214113] Published Wed Jun 24, 2026

item.title: Spatiotemporal Migration of Antiferromagnetic Domain Walls in ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{IrO}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Ian Robinson, David Yang, Ross Harder, Dina Sheyfer, Longlong Wu, Jack Griffiths, Emil Bozin, Mark P. M. Dean, Jialun Liu, Hengdi Zhao, Gang Cao, Angel Rodriguez-Fernandez, Jan-Etienne Pudell, Roman Shayduk, James Wrigley, Alexey Zozulya, Rustam Rysov, Aliaksandr Leonau, Ulrike Boesenberg, Joerg Hallmann, and Anders Madsen

While laser-driven demagnetization and magnetic x-ray scattering in Sr2IrO4 are both well-established, the combination in a pump-probe experiment at an x-ray free electron laser is new. Micron-sized antiferromagnetic domains have been seen by other methods, but not the more subtle phase domains to which coherent scattering is sensitive. By measuring the temporal and spatial changes in the domain walls in a single experiment, the authors are able to establish here a wall-migration velocity for the first time.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L220410] Published Wed Jun 24, 2026

item.title: Anharmonic Collective Oscillations and Gap Generation from Thermal Fluctuations in Isotropic Classical Spin Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Anna Fancelli, Matías G. Gonzalez, Subhankar Khatua, Bella Lake, Michel J. P. Gingras, Jeffrey G. Rau, and Johannes Reuther

Here, the authors reveal a general mechanism by which soft quartic spin modes in isotropic spiral magnets generate a fluctuation-induced spin-wave gap. Unlike conventional spin waves from harmonic modes, the gap in these systems grows with temperature according to a characteristic power law. The results extend the theory of soft-mode dynamics and pseudo-Goldstone modes and provide experimentally testable predictions for neutron scattering studies of magnetic materials that do not rely on fine-tuned ground-state degeneracies.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L220411] Published Tue Jun 23, 2026

item.title: Bound States to the Continuum: Time-Varying Spoof Acoustic Surface Waves
item.description:
Author(s): E. Paul, G. J. Chaplain, J. Li, T. A. Starkey, and S. A. R. Horsley

We develop a theoretical framework for time-modulated acoustic metasurfaces comprising a line array of modulated cavities, and show that bound acoustic surface waves can undergo temporal diffraction from bound states localized at an interface into bulk waves. The dispersion relation is derived via a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 224317] Published Tue Jun 23, 2026

item.title: Triangular ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg Antiferromagnet in a Magnetic Field
item.description:
Author(s): T. Bader, S. Feng, S. Budaraju, F. Becca, J. Knolle, and F. Pollmann

The behavior of the paradigmatic ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ triangular lattice Heisenberg antiferromagnet in a magnetic field remains unsettled despite decades of study. We map out the phase diagram using three complementary approaches, including self-consistent nonlinear spin-wave theory, density-mat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L241114] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Bond-Rigidity Heterogeneity Induced Anomalous Strong Bond–strong Anharmonicity and Coherent Phonon Transport in the Ultralow Lattice Thermal Conductivity Compounds $X\mathrm{Ag}Y (X=\mathrm{K},\mathrm{Rb},\mathrm{Cs};Y=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Se},\mathrm{Te})$
item.description:
Author(s): Xinrui Li, Yinchang Zhao, Pengfei Sui, Jun Ni, and Zhenhong Dai

Understanding the microscopic origin of intrinsically low lattice thermal conductivity is crucial for the design of thermoelectric materials. Here, the lattice dynamics and thermal transport of layered $X\mathrm{Ag}Y (X=\mathrm{K},\mathrm{Rb},\mathrm{Cs};Y=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Se},\mathrm{Te})$ compou…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 214316] Published Wed Jun 17, 2026

item.title: Influence of Lattice Anharmonicity and Structural Phase Transitions on the Fröhlich Interaction in $\mathrm{CsPbB}{\mathrm{r}}_{3}$: A First-Principles Study
item.description:
Author(s): Zeli Xu, Simon Thébaud, Marios Zacharias, Laurent Pedesseau, Claudine Katan, and Jacky Even

All-inorganic lead halide perovskites $\mathrm{CsPb}{\mathrm{X}}_{3}$ (X = Cl, Br, I) exhibit rich structural dynamics, with vibrational properties and electron-lattice interactions still under active investigation due to strong lattice anharmonicity and structural phase transitions. Here we present…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 235206] Published Wed Jun 17, 2026

item.title: Deciphering the Absence of Charge Density Waves in the V-Based Kagome Metal $\mathrm{Cs}{\mathrm{V}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Rongxiao Du, Yunfan Yang, Wenxin Lv, Junhong Yu, Hang Zhang, Hechang Lei, and Jianbo Hu

While charge density waves (CDWs) are a hallmark of the kagome metal $\mathrm{Cs}{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$, their absence in the bilayer counterpart $\mathrm{Cs}{\mathrm{V}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$ remains a notable exception, with the underlying mechanism yet to be elucidated. Here, integrat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 245119] Published Tue Jun 09, 2026

item.title: Anharmonic Quantum Effects of Implanted Muons: Route to Probing Nuclear Quantum Behavior in Solids
item.description:
Author(s): F. Hotz, M. Gomilšek, T. Arh, T. J. Hicken, P. Umek, A. Zorko, and H. Luetkens

The quantum behavior of light particles in solids gives rise to phenomena that cannot be captured by a classical description. We show that muon spin spectroscopy ($μ\mathrm{SR}$), when paired with a quantum-mechanical treatment of the implanted muon, becomes a sensitive and direct probe of nuclear q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 224420] Published Mon Jun 08, 2026

item.title: Ab Initio Superfluid Weight and Superconducting Penetration Depth
item.description:
Author(s): Kaja H. Hiorth, Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Théo Cavignac, Kristjan Haule, Miguel A. L. Marques, and Päivi Törmä

Machine learning and high-throughput screening approaches to superconductor discovery require physically meaningful descriptors that capture essential physics while remaining computationally tractable. The superfluid weight is an ideal descriptor as it is a prerequisite for superconductivity, determ…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 224509] Published Fri Jun 05, 2026

item.title: Anomalous Enhancement of Thermal Radiation Transport by Quasidisorder
item.description:
Author(s): Cheng-Long Zhou, Xin-Yu Jia, Shui-Hua Yang, Yan Wang, Yong Zhang, Hong-Liang Yi, and Mauro Antezza

The transition from order to disorder is conventionally regarded as detrimental to solid-state heat transfer in classical wave and quasiparticle systems. In striking contrast, we show that in near-field thermal radiation, breaking long-range order—shifting from periodic to quasiperiodic configuratio…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 245408] Published Wed Jun 03, 2026

item.title: Transferable Electronic Structure Prediction via Slater-Koster Constrained Graph Attention Neural Network
item.description:
Author(s): Xiangyang Zhu, Tengfei Wang, Hao Wang, and Sheng Chang

Developing machine learning models that generalize across diverse chemical compositions remains a fundamental challenge in electronic structure theory. Conventional data-driven approaches often exhibit limited transferability due to their reliance on memorizing potential energy surfaces of specific …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 235103] Published Tue Jun 02, 2026

item.title: Quantum Geometric Magnetic Monopole and Two-Phase Superconductivity in ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kosuke Nogaki and Youichi Yanase

Recent angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) and density functional theory plus Hubbard $U$ ($\mathrm{DFT}+U$) studies revealed that a heavy-fermion superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRh}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ exhibits Van Hove singularities and the Dirac point near the Fermi level ${E}_{\mathr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 214506] Published Mon Jun 01, 2026
